name: Tula Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the courage of Tula's defenders and the historic colour of the governorate
purity, peace and the silver of the city's metalworking tradition

description: A vertical triband of red, white and red with a slightly broader white centre; the coat of arms of Tula Oblast is placed at the centre.
history: The flag of Tula Oblast was adopted on 24 February 2000. The arms recall Tula's historic identity as the great Russian centre of firearms production since the 16th century.

features.symbolism: the red flanks recall the historic Tula governorate banner and the bravery of the city's defenders; the central white panel carries the regional arms.
two crossed silver gun barrels and a sword over a red field, the historic Tula arms recalling the city's centuries-old role as Russia's principal arms-manufacturing centre.
